items.subject: Supa Update April 2026
items.body_text:
[Ant at Supabase]
Everything that happened in the last month at Supabase  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ 

Supabase Update — April 2026

Hey there 👋,
Here’s everything that happened with Supabase in the last month:

Multigres Operator is now open source

The Multigres Kubernetes operator is now open source, with direct pod management, zero-downtime rolling upgrades, pgBackRest PITR backups, and OTel tracing.
[GitHub] [Twitter]

The State of Startups 2026

We're running the State of Startups 2026 survey again. 10 minutes on what you're building, what stack you're using, and how AI is changing both. Fill it out and we'll send you a free t-shirt once we publish the results.
[Fill out the survey]

GitHub integration on all plans

GitHub integration is now available on all plans. Connect your repo on the free tier to deploy migrations from your main branch via CI/CD, no branching required.
[GitHub]

Supabase docs over SSH

Browse all Supabase docs with standard Unix tools, or pipe them directly into Claude Code: ssh supabase.sh setup | claude .
[Blog]

Supabase Security Newsletter

Subscribe to the Supabase security newsletter, sent only when there are important security updates.
[Sign Up]

 

Quick Product Announcements

Studio now has "Fix with Assistant" buttons across touchpoints, with a dropdown to send the prompt to Claude or ChatGPT. [Twitter]
Browser tabs now show your exact navigation path so you can tell your tabs apart at a glance. [Twitter]
Supabase secret keys now have Push Protection on GitHub, blocking accidental commits before they land. [Twitter]
Schema Visualiser: relation lines are now clickable, tables and columns have context actions, and popovers appear between connected tables. [Link]

Meet the Supabase team

Sugu Sougoumarane presents Multigres at CMU's "PostgreSQL vs. The World" seminar, April 13, free on Zoom. [Register]
Accenture AI ReinventionX, April 20, London [Register]
Sugu Sougoumarane and Deepthi Sigireddi present "Multigres: One Stop PostgreSQL Management and Scaling" at Postgres Conference, April 23, San Jose. Use code 2026_SUPABASE20  for 20% off. [Register]
Stripe Sessions, April 29-30, San Francisco [Register]
Prompt to Prod: Debug Your Full Supabase Stack with Sentry, workshop, April 30 at 10am PT [Register]

Made with Supabase

Menugo - AI-powered QR code menu generator for restaurants [Website]
Gasindex - AI voice agents call thousands of businesses to track real-time gas prices in America with Vision AI and crowdsourced submissions keeping data fresh [Website]
Guinndex - AI voice agents call thousands of businesses to track real-time Guinness prices in Ireland with Vision AI and crowdsourced submissions keeping data fresh [Website]
Festie - The unofficial Coachella 2026 companion [Website]
burn0 - Track supabase costs per-request in real time [GitHub]

Community Highlights

I Dare You to Fact-Check Me: You Need Supabase Before You Build Anything Else with AI [Blog]
Supabase in Production: What I Actually Learned Building Real Features [Blog]
I Got Surprised By A GitHub Actions Quota. Built A Tool To Make Sure It Never Happens Again, Here's How I Built It [Blog]
The Solo Dev Cheat Code: Building Fast with Next.js, Supabase, and Vercel in 2026 [Blog]

We're Hiring

Come join one of the fastest-growing open source projects ever 🤗
We are hiring across all departments. Here are the Jobs of the Month:

Customer Solutions Architect
Postgres Engineer
Developer Relations Engineer 

⚠️ Baking hot meme zone ⚠️

 

©2026 Supabase Inc.
3500 S. DuPont Highway, Ken 19901, Dover, Delaware, USA

The email was sent to anelson@cloudops.com. To no longer receive these emails, unsubscribe here.

items.subject: Get Prioritized Signals That Find Your Next Deal

You're Missing Your Most Important Feature

Can't prioritize which accounts to focus on? Missing proactive signals, notifications, and action prompts, while manually tracking accounts when automation exists?

items.body_text:
[Jorge Quintero]
Deployment has completed. Everything is working find.
When working in SFDC, if you see something not working or out of place, please reach out to me.

Jorge Quintero
Director, Business Intelligence
+1 (305) 717-6674
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Sent: Saturday, April 11, 2026 4:47:28 PM
To: Commercial Sales <commercial@aptum.com>
Cc: Vicki Patten <Vicki.Patten@aptum.com>; Emma Kopang <Emma.Kopang@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Ikram Nagdawala <Ikram.Nagdawala@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: SalesForce Development 
 

Deployment started at 4:30 PM

Jorge Quintero
Director, Business Intelligence
+1 (305) 717-6674
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, April 10, 2026 9:37:05 PM
To: Commercial Sales <commercial@aptum.com>
Cc: Vicki Patten <Vicki.Patten@aptum.com>; Emma Kopang <Emma.Kopang@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Ikram Nagdawala <Ikram.Nagdawala@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: SalesForce Development 
 

Deployment will begin at 3 pm, and it will take about 3 hours.

Jorge Quintero
Director, Business Intelligence
+1 (305) 717-6674
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, April 10, 2026 1:32:25 PM
To: Commercial Sales <commercial@aptum.com>
Cc: Vicki Patten <Vicki.Patten@aptum.com>; Emma Kopang <Emma.Kopang@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Ikram Nagdawala <Ikram.Nagdawala@aptum.com>
Subject: SalesForce Development 
 

Hi,

As some of you may be aware, over the past few weeks we have been working on cleaning up our Salesforce environment. As a first phase, we will remove a number of unused Apex and metadata components that have accumulated over the years as various initiatives were introduced and later retired. This activity will have no impact on day-to-day operations; no current functionality will be added, changed, or removed.

This cleanup will be performed in the production environment tomorrow, Saturday the 11th. The process is expected to take approximately two to three hours, during which time the platform will be unavailable. I will share the specific timing later today, and tomorrow I will send a brief notification when the work begins and another once it is completed.
This is a very low-risk initiative. The process has been successfully executed in the UAT environment and thoroughly QA tested, with no issues identified. Many thanks to @Ikram Nagdawala and @Steve Rioux for their support throughout this effort. In the unlikely event that an issue arises, a rollback plan is in place.

No action is required from your side; this message is shared for awareness in case you were planning to use Salesforce tomorrow.

Please feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions.

Jorge Quintero
Director, Business Intelligence
+1 (305) 717-6674
aptum.com | LinkedIn

---

items.subject: Your Professional Services questions, answered in one place
items.body_text:
[TSIA]
Webinars, research, and real-world guidance, available to you.
 

 View this email in your browser 

Hi Adam,
If you’re responsible for Professional Services, you’re likely navigating a familiar set of challenges:
Preparing your PS organization for what’s next.
Modernizing delivery without disrupting today’s business.
Evolving offers, operations, and team capabilities in a way that actually scales.
The good news? You don’t have to piece together guidance from scattered opinions and one-off LinkedIn takes.
Inside the TSIA Portal, Professional Services leaders have access to research, on-demand sessions, and practical frameworks designed for real-world execution, not theory. Here are three popular resources PS teams are using right now:

 
The Future of Professional Services & How to Prepare For It
In this session, TSIA’s Bo DiMuccio and Kantata’s Fortuné Alexander walk through the major shifts reshaping PS—and what leaders should start building now. You’ll get a clear view of what’s changing across strategy, operating models, tooling, and customer expectations—and how to prepare without waiting for “the perfect time.” View in the TSIA Portal 

 
Report: The State of Professional Services 2026
This flagship report outlines what Professional Services leaders are facing in 2026—what’s accelerating, what’s stalling, and where execution maturity is lagging. It’s designed to help leaders understand the key trends, business challenges, and strategic imperatives shaping PS decisions this year.Explore insights 

 
What Is Professional Services 2.0?
If “PS 2.0” is showing up in your conversations but still feels abstract, this article makes it tangible. It breaks down what PS 2.0 actually means, how it differs from traditional project-based delivery, and the core elements PS organizations need to evolve across strategy, operations, and delivery design. Read the article 

All of this content, and so much more, is available when you create a TSIA portal account.
With a free account, you can:
Watch on-demand webinars.
Read select research and reports.
Explore how peers are evolving Professional Services.

Create your free TSIA Account 

If you’re looking for clear, research-backed guidance, not theory, the TSIA portal is a great place to start.

            

This email was sent by: TSIA
17065 Camino San Bernardo, Suite 150 San Diego, CA, 92127, US
Update Profile | Manage Subscriptions | Unsubscribe

items.subject: Sales Forecast & Pipeline - April 10th Update
items.body_text:
[Tom Kelsch]
Hi team,

The following is a summary of the Sales Forecast & Pipeline for Q3. Changes from last week's figures are noted. All figures in CAD.

Notable changes to opportunities in the past week are detailed below.

Closed Won

No notable wins

Closed Lost

Rational Exponent Inc - $7K MRC, $21K NRC - New Logo, Hyperscale + MS/PS

Newly Created

Trace Associates - $5K MRC - New Logo, Hosting, FY27

Chicken Farmers of Ontario - $5K MRC - Install Base, Manages Services, FY27

Close Date Pushed

Croesus - $60K MRC (Install Base, Hosting, pushed from May to Jun)

Upper Canada Forest - $8K MRC, $7K NRC (Install Base, Managed Services, pushed from Jun to Aug)

***

To see performance against targets, opportunity details, or other sales analysis please refer to the Power BI Dashboards (updated daily):

Sales Forecasting & Analysis - Power BI Dashboards

Thanks,

Tom Kelsch
Senior Decision Support Analyst
aptum.com | LinkedIn

items.subject: See How GTM Studio Ranks Your Best Opportunities in Minutes
items.body_text:
[The ZoomInfo Team]
Hi there,

Your GTM Studio workspace is ready, and 2,000 AI credits are waiting to be put to work.

GTM Studio helps you instantly rank your accounts based on buying intent and real-time AI research, so you know exactly where to focus.

Access GTM Studio Now

Here's how it works:

Start with your existing account list
Add buying signals and AI-powered attributes
See your highest-priority accounts rise to the top

Your 2,000 credits, explained:

Web Research enrichments
Advanced AI-powered research features

Standard enrichments, including intent signals and ZoomInfo attributes, are included.

Learn how to enrich your audiences and prioritize accounts faster.

Learn More

Turn data into coordinated go-to-market action. Join the GTM Studio Foundations webinar live this week or watch a recording on demand. Enroll Now.

Contact Us | Resources

330 W Columbia Way, Floor 8, Vancouver, WA 98660

items.subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
items.body_text:
[Andy Petterson]
Hi All,

Apologies for the delay, I'm ill and I think Jason has also been out.

Samar has confirmed that we do not need SA in this specific scenario, and during our next SPLA audit we should supply the MS auditor with the following:

Invoices of their SQL server and CAL license purchases (I think we have these already).

A signed legal letter that states that the customer knows they are responsible for making sure they have the correct number of SQL CALs, and are liable for any and all fines that Microsoft make on their behalf. I don't think this is strictly needed as Samar from Insight said "they won't chase you for this", but she said it would be good to have. Anyone we can get from legal to do this?
The only other concern is that they have only purchased 1x User/Device CAL for each server. It's extremely unlikely (but not impossible) that there is only 1 user/device that is using each SQL server, so we should go back and ask them to clarify exactly how many users/devices they are actually using. I think that they are assuming they only need to license the one server with the CAL and not the users that then access it, but they actually have to license each end user/device that they are using. Sharing the one server is called "Multiplexing" and it is not allowed.

This is a good diagram below that covers it here that we can send on to them: http://download.microsoft.com/download/8/7/3/8733d036-92b0-4cb8-8912-3b6ab966b8b2/multiplexing.pdf

We should either have a meeting to explain or email them saying that apologies for the delay but we've been going back and forth with our MS licensing partner and our legal team and although it's against company policy we can make an exception in their case, but we do need them to verify their CAL usage and confirm they aren't multiplexing.

We can then either assist with buying more CAL's (we could ask for a list of user names), or just trust that they'll purchase the right amount as it's their liability.

Regards,

Andy Petterson
Solutions Architect
+442380926091
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Hi All,

I know that we've settled on telling the customer that SA is required as per our terms and conditions because of the admin overhead and risk, but I finally got an answer back from Samar at Insight confirming SA is not required for this customer by Microsoft. This is because the hardware is dedicated to the customer, and the server is physical. See screenshot below and attached thread if interested.

On the customer call they also said that they had a Microsoft audit that looked at their SQL setup and confirmed they had the appropriate licenses and have added this to a Cherwell ticket. I have not verified this on the ticket but can do if needed.

From: Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Sent: March 19, 2026 08:57
To: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>
Cc: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
 
The licensing problem involves the product key used to install SQL Server.  Microsoft gives the exact same product key to all Software Assurance (Enterprise)/MSDN (Developer)/SPLA (Hosting) customers.  Only retail product keys are unique.  Thus, in most cases, the product key is not proof of licensing.  In past audits, if Microsoft found this product key used for SQL Server, it assumed we provided the product key and thus licensing.  If we weren’t sending Microsoft their royalty for the license, we owed back licensing fees.
 
SQL Server + CAL is a retail-only product.  It is, to my knowledge, not offered under Software Assurance, nor can it offered under SPLA.
 
If a customer buys their own SQL Server licenses through Software Assurance, they have the ability to use these licenses in Azure, other public cloud, or a hosting provider.  This is done through License Mobility and requires the license holder to submit a form attesting that they have the required license.
 
Microsoft licensing is complex and my understanding is not complete.  However, since it carries a real financial liability if we get it wrong, I believe it’s important that we understand what Microsoft allows and requires.  I’ve documents that I’ve used to make sense of this.
 

Charles Rutledge
System Specialist
+1 (678) 365-2811
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>

Cc: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
 
Hi All,
 
Just going through the thread, checking the ticket and adding Carlton to see if he has an opinion.
 
Firstly, I don't think we are in trouble here - I think we are only responsible if we supplied them with SPLA SQL licensing and we've used the wrong number of cores or incorrect edition etc. There are no SPLA licenses on their server in Ocean, so I don't think Microsoft would even check it on an audit. If they found SQL on it we could say that the customer is using BYOL and is responsible for their own licensing (we have tickets as proof we've asked), and then they would have to produce their licenses to MS and be fined if they aren't covered correctly. @Carlton, have I got that correct?
 
Secondly, my understanding is that Charles may be incorrect (apologies Charles!), not sure if there was a recent change with the licensing models. My understanding is if this was a virtual server that can be moved between hosts, or is on a shared stack, or on our/their private cloud, or a newer version of SQL than what they are licensed for, the customer would 100% need SA on their licenses and would have to use the per core model based on the cores in the physical hosts. With Azure Arc or CSP licensing, you can now get away with just licensing the cores in the VM.
 
However, this is physical hardware dedicated to them with a Windows OS directly on top with presumably a fixed number of SQL connections, so they are allowed to use the Server license + CAL model, unless I'm missing something. @Charles was that information directly from an MS ticket? If not, we should probably open one to confirm if that model is allowed on a dedicated physical server in our data centers - I think it is now allowed.
 
In answer to the other questions, If their current licensing is confirmed as inadequate, when I compared the numbers SQL Arc would probably offer the cheapest SQL licensing for them as they can pay by the hour on their Azure consumption. If they want fixed costs, we can sell them CSP 1 year or 3 year options but the 1 year would be more expensive than Arc and the 3 year is about the same, so long as MS don't jack up their SQL PAYG pricing.
 
Regards,
 

Andy Petterson
Solutions Architect
+442380926091
aptum.com | LinkedIn

 
 
From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 20:16
To: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>; Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>
Cc: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
 
All,
 
I want to close the loop on this. This ticket has been open for nearly a year, it's been passed around between multiple people (several of whom are no longer here). 
 
A few things I want to clarify up front:
 
This is not an Abuse issue. Abuse handles misuse of our services illegal activity like bitcoin mining. This is a commercial licensing gap. Fred TY, your team can stand down on this one. That said, we do need to clearly define what routes to Abuse vs. what's a commercial matter so we don't repeat this.
Someone needs to own this. @Elizabeth Perez since you're already in communication with the customer, can you take the lead? Frederic and Barbara, please support as needed from the commercial and SDM side.
The economics are what they are. Smart Scale pays us roughly $2,500/month. The compliant licensing would cost them ~$7,900/month on top of that. They're not going to pay that. Threatening suspension hasn't worked for a year and it's not going to start working now.
 
We are currently liable and at risk as an organization if we do nothing!
 
Here's what I think we should do:
 
@Elizabeth Perez, schedule a call with Mario (let's stop the back and forth email chains). He clearly doesn't understand (or doesn't want to understand) the issue, and a year of back-and-forth hasn't helped. I feel like we should present the following options:
They can purchase per-core licenses through us (the expensive path, don't lead with it, but it is an option)
They can procure Software Assurance directly from Microsoft and give us the License Mobility form (somebody to confirm... @Andy Petterson?)
We explore whether migrating their SQL workloads to Azure SQL makes sense, licensing is bundled in, the mobility problem goes away, and we potentially keep the relationship
Frederic flagged the Azure idea and I think it's worth exploring. @Andy Petterson, is it possible to get a cost comparison between the two?
If none of the above work, we agree on a timeline for them to move off our platform in an orderly way
 
Whatever comes out of the call, put the agreed path and deadline in writing. If they don't act, we follow through. No more open-ended limbo.
 
Let's get this resolved in the next two weeks. If anyone has questions or sees a gap in this, let me know.
 
Adam Nelson

VP, Operations
+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Sent: March 16, 2026 09:20
To: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Cc: Information Security <information_security@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
 
When the previous manager of Abuse (Joseph) was exited last year, a lot of historical knowledge was lost. It's also worth clarifying that he was a 1-man team; he had no reports. His Abuse responsibilities were given to InfoSec to handle as best we can, and since then, we have been trying to document some of that knowledge based on our interpretation of what Abuse should handle, which does align with what Adam said: the mis-use of our services.
 
I don't believe license (mis-)use should fall under the purview of Abuse. If a customer has installed or is using software and violating the license agreement, should Aptum be policing that at all? If we misinterpret a license agreement, couldn't that make Aptum liable? (I'm not saying Charles has misinterpreted it in this ticket, more as a general thought).
 
This ticket likely slipped through the cracks during the transition periods mentioned above, and also because, like IT, the InfoSec team don't typically look at the "Aptum" project in Jira - we have dedicated Jira projects for Security work.
 
Cheers,
Fred
 

Fred Taylor-Young
Senior Manager, IT & Security
